memberdef.argsstring: (self, str or re.Pattern regexp, str line_type, Optional[int] level_1=None, Optional[int] level_2=None, Optional[Union[bool, str]] can_be_multiline=None)
memberdef.name: __init__

briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: init method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5

detaileddescription.para: Initialize pattern with default values of :class:~dedoc.data_structures.HierarchyLevel attributes.

:param regexp: regular expression for checking, if the line text matches the pattern. Note that regular expression is used on the lowercase and stripped line. :param line_type: type of the line, e.g. "header", "bullet_list_item", "chapter", etc. :param level_1: value of a line primary importance :param level_2: level of the line inside specific class :param can_be_multiline: is used to unify lines inside tree node by :class:~dedoc.structure_constructors.TreeConstructor, if line can be multiline, it can be joined with another line. If None is given, can_be_multiline is set to True.

briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: match method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: get_hierarchy_level method \par \b I/O\/ \: Input -> Output \par \b Complexity\/ \: 5
\par \b Purpose\/ \: RegexpPattern for document structure extraction pipeline

detaileddescription.para: Pattern for matching line text by a regular expression.

.. note:: The pattern is case-insensitive (lower and upper letters are not differed). Before regular expression matching, the line text is stripped (space symbols are deleted from both sides). .. seealso:: Syntax for writing regular expressions is described in the `Python documentation <https://docs.python.org/3/library/re.html>`_. Example of library usage:

.. code-block:: python import re from dedoc.structure_extractors import DefaultStructureExtractor from dedoc.structure_extractors.patterns import RegexpPattern
reader = ... structure_extractor = DefaultStructureExtractor() patterns = [ RegexpPattern(regexp="^chapter\s\d+\.", line_type="chapter", level_1=1, can_be_multiline=False), RegexpPattern(regexp=re.compile(r"^part\s\d+\.\d+\."), line_type="part", level_1=2, can_be_multiline=False) ] document = reader.read(file_path=file_path) document = structure_extractor.extract(document=document, parameters={"patterns": patterns}) Example of API usage:
